tasks.title: Preload explicit CJK font at startup for restored tabs
tasks.description: Fix CJK rendering issue in restored tabs by preloading the user's preferred CJK font at app initialization when preference is explicitly set.
tasks.details: In app startup logic (likely `src/main.rs` or `app.rs`), read CJK font preference from settings. If explicit (non-Auto), preload the single font using same logic as Auto+system-locale preloading. Ensure font is available before restoring tabs. Update CJK font loading in `src/editor/ferrite/` or theme/font module to use preloaded font.
tasks.testStrategy: 1. Set CJK preference to explicit (Noto Sans SC). 2. Restart app with Chinese text in restored tabs. 3. Verify Chinese renders correctly in all tabs without switching. 4. Test Auto mode unchanged.

tasks.title: Implement GitHub-style callouts parsing and rendering
tasks.description: Add support for GitHub-style admonition blocks like `> [!NOTE]` with color-coded rendering and optional collapsible state.
tasks.details: Extend markdown parser in `src/markdown/parser.rs` to recognize `> [!TYPE]` and `> [!TYPE] Custom Title` syntax, store type/title/collapsed state in AST. In `src/markdown/widgets.rs`, render styled blocks with icons/colors: NOTE=blue, TIP=green, WARNING=orange, CAUTION=yellow, IMPORTANT=red. Support `> [!NOTE]-` for collapsed-by-default. Add toggle interaction preserving state per block.
tasks.testStrategy: 1. Parse/render `> [!NOTE]\n> Content` → blue note block. 2. `> [!WARNING] Custom Title` → orange with title. 3. `> [!NOTE]-` → collapsed, expands on click. 4. Verify all 5 types render correctly.

tasks.title: Add manual Check for Updates button in Settings
tasks.description: Implement Settings button to fetch latest GitHub release and show update dialog if newer version available.
tasks.details: In `src/ui/settings.rs`, add "Check for Updates" button in About/General section. Use `reqwest` (blocking/async) to fetch `https://api.github.com/repos/OlaProeis/Ferrite/releases/latest`. Parse version, compare with current using semver or string compare. Show toast/dialog with download link if newer. Handle offline/API errors with user-friendly toast.
tasks.testStrategy: 1. Click button → up-to-date toast if current. 2. Mock newer release → dialog with version/download link. 3. Offline → error toast. 4. Verify no auto-checks.

tasks.title: Implement large file detection and warning toast
tasks.description: Detect files >10MB on open and show non-blocking performance warning toast.
tasks.details: In file open flow (`app.rs` or file loading), use `std::fs::metadata(path).map(|m| m.len())` before loading. If >10MB (10*1024*1024), show toast: "Large file ({size} MB). Performance may be affected.". Proceed with load regardless. Make threshold configurable in future.
tasks.testStrategy: 1. Open 15MB file → toast shows, file opens. 2. Open <10MB → no toast. 3. Verify non-blocking.

tasks.title: Implement wikilinks parsing, resolution, and navigation
tasks.description: Add `[[wikilinks]]` and `[[target|display]]` support with relative resolution, spaces in filenames, and click navigation.
tasks.details: Extend `src/markdown/parser.rs` (pulldown-cmark/custom) to parse `[[target]]`/`[[target|display]]`, store targets. Implement resolver using Tab.content/FerriteEditor buffer: relative to current file/workspace root. Tie-breaker: same-folder-first → shortest path → prompt if ambiguous. Support spaces (`[[My Note]]` → `My Document.md`). Render clickable in split/rendered view, integrate with outline.
tasks.testStrategy: 1. `[[note-b]]`/`[[note-b|Link]]` → clicks open note-b.md, shows display text. 2. Spaces: `[[My Document]]` resolves correctly. 3. Ambiguous → prompt user choice. 4. Broken links handled gracefully.
tasks.expansionPrompt: Break down into: 1) Extend pulldown-cmark parser for [[wikilinks]] syntax capture, 2) Implement multi-level relative path resolver with same-folder priority and ambiguity handling, 3) Create clickable link rendering in markdown widgets with display text support, 4) Integrate navigation with file opening and outline updates.

tasks.title: Create backlinks panel with graph-based indexing
tasks.description: Add panel showing files linking to current file, using in-memory graph for large workspaces.
tasks.details: Create `src/ui/backlinks_panel.rs` or extend outline_panel.rs. For ≤50 files: scan on tab switch. For >50: build `HashMap<filename, Vec<referencing_files>>` on workspace load, update incrementally on save. Detect `[[current]]` and `[text](current.md)`. Click → navigate to file (scroll to link if possible). Update on tab switch/file change.
tasks.testStrategy: 1. A→B, C→B links → open B shows A,C in panel. 2. Click A → navigates to A. 3. Verify updates on save/tab switch. 4. Test performance >50 files.
tasks.expansionPrompt: Divide into: 1) Implement wikilink and markdown link parser for backlink extraction, 2) Build in-memory HashMap graph index with incremental updates on save, 3) Create backlinks UI panel with file list and click navigation, 4) Add performance scaling for >50 files with lazy scanning, 5) Integrate tab switch and file change reactivity.

tasks.title: Refactor flowchart into modular components with rendering cache
tasks.description: Split `src/markdown/mermaid/flowchart.rs` into modules and add texture caching for performance.
tasks.details: Create: `flowchart/nodes.rs` (shapes/labels), `edges.rs` (routing/arrows), `layout.rs` (positioning/rank), `render.rs` (egui drawing), `mod.rs` (API). Preserve exact behavior. Cache rendered flowchart as egui texture, recompute only on content change (hash diagram AST). Avoid layout every frame.
tasks.testStrategy: 1. All existing flowchart tests pass. 2. Manual Mermaid diagrams render identically. 3. Verify texture cache reduces CPU (profile frames).
tasks.expansionPrompt: Split into: 1) Extract nodes.rs, edges.rs, layout.rs modules preserving exact behavior, 2) Implement render.rs with egui texture caching based on AST hash, 3) Create mod.rs orchestrator API and verify performance improvements via profiling.

tasks.title: Implement native macOS window controls and icon polish
tasks.description: Add macOS traffic-light controls and ensure consistent icons across themes.
tasks.details: In `src/ui/window.rs`, configure eframe/egui for native macOS window decoration (close/min/maximize traffic lights). Verify icons scale correctly in light/dark themes following HIG. Test cross-platform consistency.
tasks.testStrategy: 1. macOS build → native traffic lights visible. 2. Icons crisp in both themes. 3. Verify Windows/Linux unchanged.
tasks.expansionPrompt: Separate into: 1) eframe/egui macOS native window decoration configuration, 2) Cross-theme icon scaling verification following HIG.

tasks.title: Implement lazy CSV row parsing with byte-offset indexing
tasks.description: For large CSVs, build row offset index and parse only visible rows for virtual scrolling.
tasks.details: In CSV viewer (`src/` CSV handling), on load: scan file building `Vec<u64>` byte offsets per row. Virtual scroll: parse only visible rows via `buffer[offsets[n]..offsets[n+1]]` using CSV parser. Keep memory low for 100MB+ files. Smooth scrolling.
tasks.testStrategy: 1. Open 100MB CSV → low memory usage. 2. Scroll → smooth, only visible rows parsed. 3. Verify row boundaries correct.
tasks.expansionPrompt: Break into: 1) Build row byte-offset index Vec<u64> on large CSV load, 2) Implement virtual scrolling parsing only visible row slices, 3) Ensure smooth scrolling and memory efficiency for 100MB+ files.

tasks.title: Add Vim mode settings toggle and status bar indicator
tasks.description: Implement optional Vim mode toggle in settings with mode status display.
tasks.details: In `src/config/settings.rs`, add vim_mode: bool (default: false). In FerriteEditor (`src/editor/ferrite/`), read setting. Add status bar showing `[NORMAL]`, `[INSERT]`, cursor shape changes. Essential keys: hjkl, dd, yy, p, i, Esc, v/V, /search.
tasks.testStrategy: 1. Toggle on/off → status bar updates. 2. Default: disabled. 3. Verify mode indicator visible.

tasks.title: Implement Vim mode core modal state machine and key handling
tasks.description: Add Normal/Insert/Visual modes with essential Vim commands and key conflict resolution.
tasks.details: In `src/editor/ferrite/` or `editor/vim.rs`: modal state machine. Normal: hjkl movement, dd/delete line, yy/yank, p/paste, /search. Insert: normal typing, Esc→Normal. Visual: v char/V line selection, y yank. Key handling: Vim consumes editor keys when active; egui shortcuts (Ctrl+C) take precedence outside Vim keys. Focus priority.
tasks.testStrategy: 1. Normal: hjkl moves, dd deletes line. 2. i→Insert, type, Esc→Normal. 3. v→select, y→yank, p→paste. 4. Ctrl+C still works globally.
tasks.expansionPrompt: Divide into: 1) Modal state machine (Normal/Insert/Visual) architecture, 2) Normal mode movement/commands (hjkl, dd, yy, p, /), 3) Insert/Visual mode handling with transitions, 4) Key conflict resolution with egui shortcuts, 5) Cursor shape and selection integration.

tasks.title: Add opt-in executable code blocks with Run button
tasks.description: Implement Run button for shell/python code blocks with security warning and settings.
tasks.details: In `src/markdown/widgets.rs`, add settings: code_execution_enabled (default:false), timeout_secs:30. First run: show security dialog, store acknowledged. Render ▶ button on ```bash```python blocks when enabled. Click→execute.
tasks.testStrategy: 1. Default: button hidden/disabled. 2. Enable → button appears. 3. First click → security dialog once. 4. Verify settings toggle works.

tasks.title: Implement code block execution engine with CWD and timeout
tasks.description: Execute shell/python scripts in file's directory with 30s timeout and platform detection.
tasks.details: Create `src/code_execution/` module. Use `std::process::Command`: Shell/Bash→Unix:`sh`/`bash`, Windows:`powershell`/`cmd` or detect `bash.exe`. Python→`python`/`python3`. CWD=file dir (fallback app root). Timeout: `std::thread::spawn` + kill after 30s. Output inline/collapsible below block.
tasks.testStrategy: 1. ```bash echo $PWD``` → shows file dir. 2. Infinite loop → killed at 30s. 3. Windows: runs via powershell/cmd. 4. Relative paths work.

tasks.title: Add welcome view on first run with basic settings
tasks.description: Show a welcome screen (modal or full view) on first launch or when user has not dismissed it, with word wrap, default view mode (Raw/Rendered/Split), optionally theme/font; 'Do not show again' stored in settings; re-open via Help or Settings.
tasks.details: Add welcome view in app startup or central UI. Options: word wrap toggle, default view (Raw/Rendered/Split), optionally theme or font size. Checkbox 'Do not show again'; persist as welcome_shown or skip_welcome in config/settings. Show only when no existing settings (first launch) or flag not set. Allow 'Show welcome' from Help or Settings. Use existing Settings struct and persistence.
tasks.testStrategy: 1. First run (no config) → welcome shows. 2. Set options, check Do not show again → next launch no welcome. 3. Re-open from Help/Settings → welcome shows. 4. Settings persist.

tasks.title: Open file in current window as tab (not new window)
tasks.description: When double-clicking a file (file tree or OS), open it as a tab in the current Ferrite window instead of opening a new window.
tasks.details: Ensure file open from file tree (src/ui/file_tree.rs → file_clicked, src/app/mod.rs handling) and from OS (double-click in Explorer / file manager) opens in current window as tab. On Windows/Linux, double-click may spawn new process; consider single-instance or passing path to existing instance. CLI args in main.rs open files as tabs; verify file tree click uses state.open_file() and does not spawn new process.
tasks.testStrategy: 1. Double-click file in file tree → opens as tab in current window. 2. Open file from OS (Explorer double-click) → opens in existing window as tab when possible. 3. No extra Ferrite window per file.
tasks.expansionPrompt: Split into: 1) Fix file tree double-click to use existing window tab opening, 2) Implement single-instance protocol for OS file associations across platforms.

tasks.title: Windows MSI: optional file associations with user choice
tasks.description: During Windows MSI install, ask user whether to set Ferrite as default for .md, .txt, .json, etc.; support 'default for all' or per-extension.
tasks.details: In wix/main.wxs (or WiX UI flow), add optional installer page or checkbox: 'Set Ferrite as default for supported file types (.md, .txt, .json, .yaml, .toml)'. Options: default for all, or per-extension selection. Do not force associations without user consent. Register ProgIds/associations only when user opts in.
tasks.testStrategy: 1. Run MSI → see file association prompt. 2. Opt in → associations set. 3. Opt out → no associations. 4. Verify .md/.txt/.json open with Ferrite when chosen.
tasks.expansionPrompt: Separate into: 1) WiX installer UI page/checkbox for optional per-extension associations, 2) ProgId registration logic only when user opts in.

tasks.title: Fix images not displaying in rendered markdown view
tasks.description: Images in markdown (![](path)) do not show in rendered/split view; fix so images display correctly.
tasks.details: In src/markdown/ (editor.rs or widgets.rs), resolve image paths relative to document location, load image bytes, render with egui Image. Support common formats (PNG, JPEG, GIF, WebP). Handle missing files and unsupported formats gracefully (placeholder or alt text). Ensure path resolution uses current file directory and workspace root.
tasks.testStrategy: 1. Rendered view: ![](./img.png) and ![](assets/logo.png) show image. 2. Missing file → placeholder or alt. 3. Split view shows images.
tasks.expansionPrompt: Divide into: 1) Implement relative path resolution for markdown images from file/workspace root, 2) egui Image rendering with format support and error handling.

tasks.title: Add German and Japanese to language settings
tasks.description: Expose German and Japanese UI languages in Settings; locale files de.yaml and ja.yaml already exist.
tasks.details: In src/config/settings.rs: add Language::German (locale_code "de", native_name "Deutsch") and Language::Japanese (locale_code "ja", native_name "日本語"). Update Language::all(), locale_code(), native_name(), and from_locale_code() to include "de" and "ja". Serde rename as needed (e.g. zh-Hans style). Settings combo in src/ui/settings.rs will pick them up automatically.
tasks.testStrategy: 1. Settings → Appearance → Language shows Deutsch and 日本語. 2. Select German → UI in German. 3. Select Japanese → UI in Japanese. 4. Persist and restore on restart.

tasks.title: Always show view mode bar for all editor tabs
tasks.description: When default view is Split and user opens a file that does not support split view (e.g. .rs), the view mode bar is hidden so mode can only be changed via hotkeys. Always display the view mode bar; for unsupported file types show the two-mode segment (Raw | Rendered).
tasks.details: In src/app/title_bar.rs the view mode segment is only shown when current_file_type is markdown, structured, or tabular (line ~320). Change to always show for has_editor && !is_special_tab. For file types that support split (markdown, tabular) use segment.show() (3-mode); for structured use segment.show_two_mode(); for all other types (e.g. .rs) also use show_two_mode(ui, current_view_mode, is_dark) so users can switch to Raw or Rendered from the UI. Optionally in open-file flow: when opening a file that does not support split and default_view_mode is Split, set tab.view_mode to Raw so initial state is consistent.
tasks.testStrategy: 1. Set default view to Split. 2. Open a .rs file → view mode bar visible with Raw | Rendered. 3. Switch to Raw and Rendered via bar. 4. Open .md file → 3-mode bar (Raw | Split | Rendered) still works. 5. Hotkey toggle still works for all.

tasks.title: Fix light mode readability (settings, menus, headlines)
tasks.description: Several UI elements are not visible in light mode (only readable in dark mode). Affected areas include the settings panel (maximum line width, tab size, other controls), menu/settings headlines and text, and possibly other panels.
tasks.details: Override_text_color and theme-aware semantic colors were tried but did not resolve the issue. Investigate root cause: (1) Where are text/headline colors set (egui styles, custom painting)? (2) Do any widgets or sections bypass theme or use non-theme-aware colors? (3) Check ui.heading(), RichText, Slider/ComboBox/DragValue value text, and any custom galley/painter text. Ensure all labels, headings, controls, and menu text have sufficient contrast in both light and dark themes. Audit settings panel, gear menu, and any other panels that use headings or styled text.
tasks.testStrategy: 1. Switch to light theme. 2. Open Settings (gear) and verify all section titles, labels, slider values, combo text, and controls are readable. 3. Check menus and any other UI with headlines. 4. Switch to dark theme and confirm no regressions. 5. Document which code paths needed changes.

tasks.title: Fix macOS Release .app Bundle packaging in CI
tasks.description: Update CI workflow to package macOS releases as proper .app bundle instead of raw binary. Issue #93: Gatekeeper blocks raw binary launches.
tasks.details: Update .github/workflows/release.yml to package target/release/bundle/osx/Ferrite.app instead of raw binary. The bundle must include: Contents/Info.plist, Contents/MacOS/ferrite binary, and Contents/Resources/. Update the tar.gz creation to include the full .app bundle structure. Verify cargo-bundle is being called before packaging. Test that the downloaded .app can be launched on macOS without Gatekeeper blocking.
tasks.testStrategy: 1. Trigger release workflow or test locally with cargo bundle --release. 2. Verify Ferrite.app is created with correct structure. 3. Test on macOS: double-click launches without Gatekeeper warning. 4. Verify Windows/Linux releases unaffected.
tasks.expansionPrompt: Break into: 1) Update release.yml to use cargo-bundle output, 2) Verify .app bundle structure and Gatekeeper compliance.

tasks.title: Implement Basic Visual Frontmatter Editor
tasks.description: Create side panel for editing YAML frontmatter as key-value pairs with form-like interface. Issue #94.
tasks.details: Create new panel in src/ui/ for frontmatter editing. Parse YAML frontmatter from markdown files (content between --- delimiters at start). Display as form with key-value pairs. Support string, date, and list (tags) field types with appropriate widgets (TextEdit, Date picker, tag list). Include 'New File Templates' feature: optional frontmatter templates when creating new markdown files. Hook into file creation flow. Panel should update live as user types in raw editor.
tasks.testStrategy: 1. Open markdown with frontmatter → panel shows key-value pairs. 2. Edit in panel → raw YAML updates. 3. Edit in raw → panel updates. 4. Create new file with template → frontmatter auto-inserted. 5. Test date picker, tag list widgets.
tasks.expansionPrompt: Break into: 1) Create frontmatter panel UI with YAML parsing, 2) Implement form widgets for string/date/list types, 3) Add two-way sync between panel and raw editor, 4) Create new file template system.

tasks.title: Add Settings UI for Complex Script Font Preferences
tasks.description: Extend CJK font preference dropdown or add 'Additional Scripts' section for users to pre-select fonts for complex scripts.
tasks.details: In src/ui/settings.rs, extend the existing CJK font preference section or create new 'Additional Scripts' section. Allow users to pre-select fonts for: Arabic, Bengali, Devanagari, Thai, Hebrew, Tamil, Georgian, Armenian, Ethiopic, other Indic, Southeast Asian scripts. Use existing font loading infrastructure in src/fonts.rs. Settings should persist and trigger lazy loading when files with those scripts are opened. Can be dropdown per script or multi-select depending on UI design preference.
tasks.testStrategy: 1. Open Settings → Editor → see Complex Scripts section. 2. Select fonts for various scripts. 3. Open file with Arabic text → font loads and renders. 4. Restart app → preferences persist. 5. Verify fonts only load when needed.
tasks.expansionPrompt: Split into: 1) Design and implement settings UI for script font selection, 2) Integrate with existing lazy font loading system.

tasks.title: Investigate and fix wrapped line scroll stuttering
tasks.description: Scrolling through documents with many word-wrapped lines shows micro-stuttering. Profile and fix the root cause.
tasks.details: Profile the editor during scroll on documents with heavy word wrapping (e.g., long paragraphs). Likely causes: per-line galley layout cost, height cache granularity issues, or excessive recomputation. Check src/editor/ferrite/view.rs for scroll handling, line_cache.rs for galley caching. Use cargo flamegraph or similar to identify hot paths. Consider: caching wrapped line heights more aggressively, reducing per-frame layout work, or optimizing the binary search in y_offset_to_line(). Document findings and implement fix.
tasks.testStrategy: 1. Profile on 1000+ line document with word wrap enabled. 2. Identify hot paths in flamegraph. 3. Implement fix. 4. Verify smooth 60fps scrolling. 5. No regression on non-wrapped documents.
tasks.expansionPrompt: Divide into: 1) Profile and identify stuttering cause, 2) Design and implement optimization, 3) Verify fix with profiling.

tasks.title: Address remaining v0.2.7 bug fixes and polish
tasks.description: General bug fixes and polish items from GitHub issues and user feedback before v0.2.7 release.
tasks.details: Review open GitHub issues tagged with 0.2.7 and any new issues. Address: IME candidate box positioning (#15), any regressions from recent changes, documentation updates for new features (wikilinks, vim mode, callouts), performance edge cases. This is a catch-all for small fixes needed before release. Update CHANGELOG.md with all changes.
tasks.testStrategy: 1. Review all open 0.2.7 tagged issues. 2. Fix or defer each. 3. Run full test suite. 4. Manual smoke test on Windows, macOS, Linux if possible. 5. Update CHANGELOG.
tasks.expansionPrompt: Break down into subtasks for: 1) Review and prioritize GitHub issues tagged 0.2.7 including IME positioning #15, 2) Fix regressions and performance edge cases, 3) Update documentation for wikilinks/vim mode/callouts, 4) Update CHANGELOG.md and run full test suite across platforms.

tasks.title: Add setting for adjustable header spacing in rendered view
tasks.description: Allow users to customize the vertical spacing between markdown headers (H1-H6) in the rendered/split view.
tasks.details: Add new setting 'header_spacing' or similar in src/config/settings.rs with options like 'compact', 'normal', 'relaxed' or a numeric value (e.g., pixels or em units). Apply this spacing in src/markdown/widgets.rs or src/markdown/editor.rs where headers are rendered. The setting should affect margin-bottom or padding after each heading level. Expose the control in Settings → Editor → Markdown Rendering. Default should be 'normal' (current behavior). Ensure the setting persists across sessions.
tasks.testStrategy: 1. Open Settings → see Header Spacing option. 2. Select 'compact' → headers have less space between them in rendered view. 3. Select 'relaxed' → more space between headers. 4. Restart app → setting persists. 5. Verify raw editor view unaffected.

tasks.title: Fix i18n translation loading for non-English languages
tasks.description: When switching to Japanese (or other non-English languages), the app displays translation keys (e.g., 'welcome.subtitle', 'welcome.section.appearance') instead of actual translated text. Fix the i18n loading so translations render correctly.
tasks.details: The issue is visible in the Welcome panel and likely affects other parts of the UI. Translation keys are shown instead of the actual Japanese/Chinese/German text. Check src/config/i18n.rs or wherever rust-i18n is initialized. Verify that: 1) locale files (ja.yaml, de.yaml, etc.) are being loaded correctly, 2) the t!() macro or equivalent is resolving keys properly, 3) the language switch triggers a reload of translations, 4) fallback to English works if a key is missing. May be related to how locale files are embedded or loaded at runtime. Check that locales/ directory is included in the build and accessible.
tasks.testStrategy: 1. Open app → Welcome panel shows in English. 2. Switch to Japanese → all text shows proper Japanese (not 'welcome.xxx' keys). 3. Switch to German → proper German text. 4. Switch to Chinese → proper Chinese. 5. Verify no regression in English. 6. Check that fallback works for missing translations.
tasks.expansionPrompt: Split into: 1) Diagnose why translation keys are displayed instead of text, 2) Fix the root cause (loading/initialization/fallback issue).

tasks.title: Fix task list checkbox rendering in markdown preview
tasks.description: Replace ASCII task list checkboxes ([ ] / [x]) with proper egui checkbox UI elements in rendered and split views, and suppress bullet point markers to avoid duplication.
tasks.details: 1. Extend MarkdownElement enum in src/markdown/parser.rs to include TaskListItem variant with fields: checked: bool, content: String. 2. Update pulldown-cmark parser in src/markdown/parser.rs to detect task list syntax: - [ ] / - [x] / * [ ] / * [x] (case-insensitive x), capture checked state and content, suppress default list rendering. 3. In src/markdown/widgets.rs, add rendering logic for TaskListItem: use egui::Checkbox with read-only state (disabled=true), style to match theme (border color, size ~14px), position checkbox before content with proper spacing (margin: 0 4px 0.25em 0). 4. Set list-style-type: none or equivalent egui styling to suppress bullet markers (no ::before bullet pseudo-element). 5. Ensure nested lists and indentation preserved (use indent level from AST). 6. Apply consistently to both rendered view and split view. 7. Handle edge cases: malformed syntax ([abc]), standalone checkboxes, mixed lists. Phase 2 (future): add click-to-toggle with buffer state preservation.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Rendered view: - [ ] Task → shows unchecked checkbox UI (no bullet). 2. - [x] Done → shows checked checkbox UI (no bullet). 3. Mixed list: normal bullet + task list → only task items show checkbox, normal items show bullet. 4. * [X] uppercase X → treated as checked. 5. Nested: - [ ] Parent
  - [x] Child → proper indentation + checkboxes. 6. Split view: identical rendering to full rendered view. 7. Malformed: - [abc] → renders as text, no crash. 8. Toggle temporarily disabled (checkboxes non-interactive).

tasks.title: Fix Open Folder functionality in Flatpak builds
tasks.description: Implement portal-based file access using xdg-desktop-portal to resolve Flatpak sandbox restrictions preventing folder opening unless a file is already open.
tasks.details:
1. Detect Flatpak environment by checking if `std::env::var("FLATPAK_ID").is_ok()` in the folder open flow (likely src/state.rs or file dialog code). [1][2]
2. For Flatpak: Use ashpd crate (add to Cargo.toml: ashpd = { version = "0.9", features = ["filechooser"] }) to invoke xdg-desktop-portal file chooser with directory selection mode via `ashpd::desktop::FileChooser::new().select_folders()`. Grant read access to selected folder(s) and update app state to open them. [1][4]
3. For non-Flatpak: Retain existing native file dialog implementation as fallback.
4. Add comprehensive error handling: If portal fails or access denied, catch ashpd errors and display user-friendly message explaining Flatpak sandbox limitations (e.g., 'Flatpak sandbox restricts folder access. Please open a file from the folder first or grant additional permissions via Flatseal.'). [1][3]
5. Ensure implementation respects Flatpak best practices: avoid static --filesystem=home permissions, rely on portals for minimal access. [2][3]
6. Update relevant handlers in src/state.rs (folder open logic), src/ui/file_dialog.rs or equivalent. Test portal response parsing to extract folder paths correctly.
7. No breaking changes to native builds - use runtime detection.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Build and install Flatpak version, verify FLATPAK_ID env var present.
2. Attempt 'Open Folder' → portal dialog appears, select folder → opens correctly (file tree shows contents, can open files within).
3. Open folder without prior file open → succeeds via portal.
4. Non-Flatpak build → uses native dialog, no portal, functions as before.
5. Simulate portal failure (mock ashpd error) → shows user-friendly sandbox explanation.
6. Open file from folder first, then another folder → both work.
7. Verify no broad filesystem permissions requested in Flatpak manifest.
8. Test on multiple DEs (GNOME, KDE) where xdg-desktop-portal implementations differ.

tasks.title: Optimize word wrap scroll performance (O(N) hot paths)
tasks.description: Address performance bottlenecks in the word wrap scroll pipeline that cause micro-stuttering on wrapped documents.
tasks.details:
Four key optimizations:

1. **Incremental rebuild_height_cache()** (view.rs): Currently iterates the entire document O(total_lines) whenever wrap_info_dirty is true (which happens when scrolling reveals new lines). Change to only recompute cumulative heights from the first changed line index forward, reusing cached prefix.

2. **O(1) LRU in LineCache** (line_cache.rs): Replace VecDeque-based LRU with LinkedHashMap or similar. Current update_lru_order() does O(N) linear scan + remove on every cache hit (up to 200 entries, 30-50 hits per frame).

3. **O(log N) visual row mapping** (view.rs): Add cumulative visual-row array alongside cumulative_heights so visual_row_to_logical() and logical_to_visual_row() use binary search instead of O(N) linear iteration.

4. **Fix get_galley_with_job cache key** (line_cache.rs): CacheKey::from_content_hash() only hashes content, ignoring font/color/theme. Different LayoutJobs for the same content can share a key, risking wrong galley returns.

Key files: src/editor/ferrite/view.rs, src/editor/ferrite/line_cache.rs
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Profile with cargo flamegraph on 1000+ line wrapped document before and after.
2. Measure frame times during continuous scroll.
3. Verify cache hit rate with metrics logging.
4. Run existing view.rs tests to confirm no regressions.
5. Test resize behavior (wrap width changes) for cache consistency.
tasks.expansionPrompt: Create one subtask per optimization: 1) Incremental rebuild_height_cache() in view.rs, 2) O(1) LRU cache replacement in line_cache.rs, 3) O(log N) visual row mapping with binary search, 4) Fix get_galley_with_job cache key collision.

tasks.title: Verify and profile word wrap scroll fix
tasks.description: Profile the word wrap scroll pipeline after Phase 1 correctness fixes and Phase 2 optimizations to verify smooth 60fps scrolling.
tasks.details:
1. Profile with cargo flamegraph on a 1000+ line document with word wrap enabled.
2. Identify any remaining hot paths in the scroll pipeline.
3. Verify 60fps scrolling is achieved (frame time < 16.6ms).
4. Test on documents with varying wrap patterns (short lines, very long paragraphs, mixed).
5. Verify no regression on non-wrapped documents.
6. Verify mouse wheel scroll feels consistent (check scroll unit calculation in editor.rs ~line 2010 vs input/mouse.rs SCROLL_LINES * line_height inconsistency).
7. Document findings and update docs/technical/editor/ if significant changes were made.

Key files: src/editor/ferrite/view.rs, src/editor/ferrite/line_cache.rs, src/editor/ferrite/editor.rs
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Flamegraph before/after comparison.
2. Frame time measurement during continuous scroll on 1000+ line wrapped doc.
3. Manual test: smooth scroll feel on various document sizes.
4. Regression test: non-wrapped documents unchanged performance.
5. Test edge cases: very long single line wrapping to many rows, empty lines mixed with long lines.
tasks.expansionPrompt: Subtasks: 1) Run flamegraph profiling on 1000+ line wrapped documents and analyze hot paths, 2) Measure frame times and verify 60fps across scroll patterns, 3) Test regressions on non-wrapped docs and document findings.

tasks.title: Implement Ctrl+Mouse Wheel Zoom for Editor with Persistence
tasks.description: Add Ctrl+Mouse Wheel zoom functionality to the editor using existing zoom logic from keyboard shortcuts and persist the zoom level in Settings.
tasks.details: 1. In `src/editor/ferrite/input/mouse.rs`, detect scroll events where Ctrl key is held (`if ctx.input().modifiers.ctrl && scroll_event.amount > 0` for zoom in, `< 0` for zoom out). Call the existing zoom functions from `src/app/keyboard.rs` (e.g., `zoom_in()` / `zoom_out()` or equivalent zoom_step(f32 delta)). Convert scroll delta to zoom steps (e.g., `delta.y * 0.1` or discrete steps matching Ctrl+/Ctrl+-). 2. Add zoom_level: f32 field to Settings struct in `src/config/settings.rs` (default: 1.0, range 0.5-3.0). Load on app startup and apply to FerriteEditor (likely via font scale or viewport scale in egui/eframe). Update zoom_level on every zoom change. 3. Persist via existing config serialization (TOML/JSON) - ensure Settings::save() called after zoom changes (debounced). 4. Apply zoom to editor rendering: scale font_size or ui.scale_factor in FerriteEditor widget. Center zoom on cursor position if possible (use mouse pos relative to editor rect). 5. Add bounds checking: clamp zoom_level 0.5-3.0, smooth transitions with lerp if multiple rapid scrolls. 6. Handle persistence across app restarts and multiple tabs/files.
tasks.testStrategy: 1. Hold Ctrl + scroll up → editor zooms in (verify font/editor larger). 2. Ctrl + scroll down → zooms out. 3. Zoom steps match Ctrl++/Ctrl+- behavior and size. 4. Close/reopen app → zoom level persists. 5. Verify zoom clamped (cannot go below 0.5x or above 3.0x). 6. Rapid scroll → smooth, no jitter. 7. Different files/tabs → zoom per editor instance or global via Settings. 8. Settings UI shows current zoom value.
tasks.expansionPrompt: Subtasks: 1) Add Ctrl+scroll detection in mouse.rs calling existing zoom logic, 2) Add zoom_level field to Settings with persistence, 3) Apply zoom scaling to editor rendering with cursor centering, 4) Add bounds checking, smoothing, and multi-tab handling.

tasks.title: Improve Linux File Dialog Error Handling for Hyprland and Portal Failures
tasks.description: Detect xdg-desktop-portal failures in file dialogs on Linux desktops like Hyprland, show helpful error messages with installation instructions, investigate rfd zenity fallback, and add documentation.
tasks.details: 1. In `src/files/dialogs.rs`, wrap rfd file dialog calls (open/save) with error detection: check if dialog returns `Err` or empty/invalid path when user selects a file. Detect Linux/Hyprland environment via `std::env::var("XDG_CURRENT_DESKTOP").contains("Hyprland")` or `"wayland"` session. 2. On failure, show egui modal dialog: 'File dialog failed. Hyprland requires xdg-desktop-portal backend for file chooser. Install `xdg-desktop-portal-hyprland` + `xdg-desktop-portal-gtk` and restart. See docs for setup.' Include package manager examples: Arch:`sudo pacman -S xdg-desktop-portal-hyprland xdg-desktop-portal-gtk`; Ubuntu:`sudo apt install xdg-desktop-portal-gtk`; Fedora:`sudo dnf install xdg-desktop-portal-gtk`. Add 'Copy instructions' button. 3. Investigate rfd 0.14+ zenity fallback: check rfd docs/changelog for `RfdOptions::with_fallback()` or similar. If available, enable automatically on Linux when portal detected as missing (e.g., via `which xdg-desktop-portal-hyprland`). Test if zenity provides acceptable UX. 4. Update `src/app/file_ops.rs` and `src/app/export.rs` to propagate dialog errors to calling functions. 5. Add documentation in `README.md` or `docs/linux-portals.md`: explain portal requirements, Hyprland limitations (no FileChooser impl), recommended packages per distro, config (`~/.config/xdg-desktop-portal/hyprland-portals.conf` with `default=hyprland;gtk`), and env vars (`XDG_CURRENT_DESKTOP=Hyprland`). Link to Hyprland wiki[2][3]. 6. Log failures to console with `log::warn!("File dialog failed on Linux: likely missing portal backend");` for debugging.
tasks.testStrategy: 1. On Hyprland without portals: trigger file open/save → error dialog shows with install instructions, copy works. 2. Verify detection: `XDG_CURRENT_DESKTOP=Hyprland` env var triggers Linux-specific message. 3. Install portals → file dialogs work normally, no error. 4. Test other desktops (GNOME/KDE): portals work, no false positives. 5. If rfd zenity fallback implemented: verify fallback dialog appears on portal failure, allows file selection. 6. Non-Wayland/X11 Linux: graceful fallback or different error. 7. Check docs: verify README contains portal setup instructions. 8. Console logs show warnings on failure.
tasks.expansionPrompt: Subtasks: 1) Detect portal failures and Hyprland env in dialogs.rs with egui error modal and copyable instructions, 2) Investigate/implement rfd zenity fallback, 3) Update file_ops.rs error propagation and add Linux portal docs.

tasks.title: Keep text selected after applying markdown formatting
tasks.description: When users apply markdown formatting (Bold, Italic, Strikethrough, etc.) to selected text, the selection is lost after the formatting markers are inserted. Extend the selection to cover the newly formatted text including syntax markers to enable chaining formatting operations.
tasks.details: The formatting logic in src/app/formatting.rs currently inserts formatting markers (e.g., wrapping selection with ** for bold) but does not update the selection afterward. To fix this: 1. After inserting opening and closing formatting markers in each formatting function (bold, italic, strikethrough, code, etc.), calculate the new selection range. The new range should span from the start of the opening marker to the end of the closing marker. 2. For example, if the original selection was at positions [5, 10] and you insert ** before and after, the new selection should be [5, 14] (assuming 2-character markers on each side). Account for marker lengths: bold/italic use 1-2 characters, strikethrough uses 2, code uses 1 backtick or 3 for blocks. 3. Call the selection management API in src/editor/ferrite/editor.rs to set the new selection range on the FerriteEditor instance. Review the Selection struct in src/editor/ferrite/selection.rs to understand the selection representation (likely start/end positions or anchor/focus). 4. Ensure the recalculation handles edge cases: empty selections (cursor position), selections at document boundaries, and nested formatting (applying formatting to already-formatted text). 5. Test with all formatting types: bold (**), italic (*), strikethrough (~~), inline code (`), and code blocks (```). Verify the selection visually highlights the formatted text including markers.
tasks.testStrategy: 1. Select text 'hello' and apply Bold → selection extends to cover **hello** (8 characters total). 2. With **hello** selected, apply Italic → selection extends to cover ***hello*** (11 characters). 3. Select text and apply Strikethrough → selection covers ~~text~~ including markers. 4. Apply inline code formatting → selection covers `text` with backticks. 5. Apply code block formatting → selection covers entire block with triple backticks. 6. Test cursor-only selection (no text selected) → formatting inserts markers and positions cursor between them. 7. Test selection at document start/end → no crashes, selection calculated correctly. 8. Verify chaining: select text, apply Bold (selection updates), immediately apply Italic without re-selecting (selection updates again to cover both markers). 9. Undo formatting → selection reverts appropriately.
tasks.expansionPrompt: Subtasks: 1) Update all formatting functions in formatting.rs to recalculate and set new selection range including markers, 2) Handle edge cases (empty sel, boundaries, nesting) and test chaining across all formats.

tasks.title: Verify and fix CJK explicit preference font preloading at startup (GitHub #76 follow-up)
tasks.description: Verify the CJK font preloading fix from Task 11 works correctly for explicit preferences in restored tabs without tofu squares; implement if broken and verify related font counter/line cache invalidation.
tasks.details: 1. Review Task 11 implementation in src/fonts.rs and src/app/mod.rs startup sequence - check if explicit CJK preference (non-Auto, e.g. Japanese/Noto Sans JP) triggers preload using same logic as Auto+system-locale before tab restoration. 2. If missing/broken: In src/app/mod.rs startup (after settings load, before tab restore), read CJK_font_preference from config; if != Auto, call preload_cjk_font(cjk_preference) matching Auto preload pattern. 3. Verify font_generation_counter increments on preload in src/fonts.rs (prevents stale font refs). 4. Test src/editor/ferrite/line_cache.rs invalidation: galleys with missing CJK glyphs should recache post-preload (check font_id matches loaded font). 5. Edge cases: explicit Japanese pref + Chinese chars (fallback), restart with multiple restored tabs, Auto unchanged. 6. If fully working across tests, mark complete. 7. Update GitHub #76 with verification results.
tasks.testStrategy: 1. Set CJK pref to Japanese (non-Auto), open CJK file (Japanese/Chinese chars), restart app → verify immediate correct rendering in ALL restored tabs (no tofu, no tab switch needed). 2. Repeat for Simplified Chinese pref. 3. Verify font_generation_counter increments (log or debug print in fonts.rs). 4. Force stale cache: edit line_cache.rs to skip invalidation temporarily → confirm tofu on restart, re-enable → clean render. 5. Regression: Auto mode unchanged, non-CJK files unaffected. 6. Multi-tab: 3+ tabs with mixed CJK → all render correctly on startup. 7. Manual inspection: no console errors, galley rebuild logs show correct font_id.
tasks.expansionPrompt: Subtasks: 1) Review Task 11 code and test explicit CJK prefs on startup/tab restore for tofu issues, 2) If broken: implement preload logic + verify font counter/line cache invalidation across edge cases.
